text: Let's visualize a forward pass through a 3-layer multi-layer perceptron.
We start with 3 input values. These represent our input features.
Each input connects to every node in the hidden layer. The values multiply by weights, sum with biases, then pass through the ReLU activation function.
The activated hidden values flow to the output layer, again multiplying by weights and adding biases.
Finally, we apply softmax to get class probabilities. The model predicts the class with the highest probability.
This is the forward pass of a neural network: matrix multiplication, bias addition, and non-linear activation, layer by layer.
